title: Objects, Packfiles, Reachability, and Integrity
description: A long-lived repository grows unexpectedly, contains unreachable objects, and reports a missing tree after interrupted maintenance; the team needs causal storage evidence before cleanup. Learners cumulatively produce object-database integrity and reachability report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace blobs, trees, commits, annotated tags, parent edges, and reference roots as an immutable reachable object graph.
Explain loose objects, pack indexes, delta bases, multi-pack indexes, and why storage representation does not change object identity.
Distinguish reachable, unreachable, dangling, promised, missing, and corrupt objects using fsck and reference evidence.
